import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ selector: 'app-about', templateUrl: './about.component.html', styleUrls: ['./about.component.scss'] }) export class AboutComponent { interests = [ { label: 'Cybersecurity', detail: 'The foundation: networks, systems, and how to defend them' }, { label: 'AI Security', detail: 'Where intelligent systems and security intersect, and the new risks that come with it' }, { label: 'Cloud Security', detail: 'Securing the infrastructure that most modern systems now run on' }, { label: 'Digital Health', detail: 'Applying secure tech thinking to healthcare and wellbeing tools' }, { label: 'Research', detail: 'Going deeper than coursework, understanding why attacks work, not just how to stop them' } ]; }
cat about.md

About Me

I've always been drawn to how things work — and, more specifically, how they break. That curiosity for problem-solving is what pulled me toward tech in the first place, and cybersecurity felt like a natural extension of it: instead of just building systems, I wanted to understand how to protect them.

That's what led me to specialise in Security & Network Engineering rather than a more general IT path. I wanted to work on the defensive side — thinking like an attacker in order to build better defences, whether that's securing a network, hardening an application, or helping people recognise when something's not right, which is a big part of why phishing awareness became the focus of my final-year research.

Where my interests have grown from there

{{ interest.label }}

{{ interest.detail }}

Outside of security specifically, I enjoy building practical tools — from network simulations to full applications — that solve real problems for real people, whether that's a family meal-planning app or a phishing simulator.
